reliability: Medium (tech influencer opinion, but reflects real user experience)
fetchStatus: YouTube content not directly fetchable; reconstructed from oEmbed metadata + web search
summary: Theo (t3.gg), a prominent tech YouTuber and developer, published a video titled 'Claude Code is unusable now' criticizing Claude Code's deteriorating usability, likely focused on the rate limit drain crisis that has affected users since March 23, 2026.

Core issue: Claude Code's prompt caching system is broken (GitHub issue #40524). Cache writes happen but reads fail, causing full reprocessing of conversation history. Users paying $100-200/month see 5-hour session windows drain in 90 minutes on identical workloads.

Anthropic admitted (April 1) that users are hitting limits 'way faster than expected.' Described as both a caching regression and 'intentional capacity management.' The bug hits hardest on agentic workflows with large codebases, exactly the Max plan use case.
Theo has also criticized Claude Code being closed-source (preventing community bug fixes) and Anthropic DMCA'ing his fork. The relationship between Anthropic and power users is strained.

Vigil relevance: Vigil runs on Claude Code and may be affected by the same rate limit drain. This could explain any session cost spikes observed since March 23. The rate limit drain bug was noted in our AI tools watchlist but this video shows it is now mainstream discourse among developers.
